sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, securing 2 wins, 2 draws, and suffering a heavy 4-0 loss to Elche. Mallorca, on the other hand, are in a slump with no wins in their last 3 matches, managing only 1 win in their last 5 (3-1 vs Elche). Both teams have struggled with consistency, but Rayo's recent away win (3-1 vs Granada) gives them a slight edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between these sides, results have been tight, with a 0-0 draw at Rayo’s home and a narrow 1-0 victory for Mallorca at their ground. Historically, Mallorca have the upper hand with no losses in the last 7 head-to-heads, often scoring first, which could play a psychological role.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano (15th, 19 points) struggle offensively, ranking 19th in goals scored (14), though they overperform their xG significantly. Mallorca (17th, 18 points) have a slightly better attacking output with 20 goals, led by Vedat Muriqi’s 10 goals, but their defense has conceded 26 goals. Rayo’s high possession (54.8%) and corner frequency (6 per game) could test Mallorca’s defensive resolve.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: For Rayo, Jorge de Frutos (5 goals) and Florian Lejeune (recent form, 7.64 rating) will be crucial in attack and defense. Mallorca rely heavily on Vedat Muriqi, who has been in fine form with 4 goals in the last 5 matches, making him a constant threat upfront.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Rayo’s strength lies in their possession play and final third entries (27.3 per game), which could exploit Mallorca’s weaker pressing (low possessions won in final third). Mallorca, however, score late (27% of goals after 76 minutes), so Rayo must stay focused until the end. Both teams’ recent games suggest a match with over 4.5 cards due to frequent fouls.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction (46% home win, 24% draw, 30% away win) and Rayo’s home advantage at Estadio de Vallecas, they are slight favorites. Mallorca’s inability to win recently and Rayo’s decent home form (including a 3-0 win vs FC Drita) tilt the balance, though a tight game is expected due to historical draws and low-scoring encounters.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at Estadio de Vallecas saw an entertaining clash between Rayo Vallecano and Mallorca, with the home side leading 2-1 at the break. Rayo Vallecano struck early through Jorge de Frutos before Mallorca responded with a header from Vedat Muriqi to level the scoreline. A late penalty converted by Isi Palazón restored Rayo’s lead, giving them the edge going into the second half. Both teams showed attacking intent, though Rayo Vallecano appeared more clinical in front of goal, capitalizing on key opportunities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Rayo Vallecano dominated the attacking momentum for large spells of the first half, particularly in the early stages with consistent pressure that led to their opening goal. They maintained frequent attacking waves, especially in the middle of the half, showing sustained intent to build play in the final third. Mallorca had their moments, with notable shifts in momentum around the time of their equalizer, pushing forward with purpose. However, their attacking spells were less frequent, and they struggled to maintain consistent pressure compared to the home side.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Rayo Vallecano showcased a strong performance in the first half, controlling possession and creating more clear-cut chances. Their ability to dominate aerial duels and overall duels reflects a physical edge over Mallorca, which likely contributed to their attacking opportunities. Mallorca, while competitive in terms of final third entries, struggled to convert their forays into meaningful chances, with fewer shots on target. Additionally, their disciplinary issues, with two yellow cards, could impact their approach in the second half if they continue to play aggressively.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Rayo Vallecano, Jorge de Frutos has been a standout performer, already on the scoresheet and posing a constant threat down the flank. Isi Palazón’s composure from the penalty spot also highlights his importance in crucial moments. On the Mallorca side, Vedat Muriqi remains their biggest danger, using his physical presence to win aerial battles and score. Toni Lato’s contribution in creating chances from the back could be vital if Mallorca are to mount a comeback.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see Rayo Vallecano continue their dominance, leveraging their home advantage and superior attacking output from the first half. They will aim to exploit Mallorca’s defensive frailties, especially in aerial duels, to extend their lead. Mallorca, however, will not go down without a fight and are expected to push for an equalizer, potentially taking more risks at the back. Their need to chase the game could open up spaces for Rayo to counterattack effectively. Given the intensity of the first half, the game might see at least a couple more goals as both teams search for a decisive result.
